title: Advanced Git 2.55: Internals, Recovery, Scale, and Production Workflows

audience.description: Developers, maintainers, release engineers, and incident responders who already use Git confidently and need production-grade repository reasoning.
entryKnowledge: Operate Git working tree, index, commits, branches, remotes, conflicts, tags, revisions, undo, rebase, hooks, and collaboration at Git Foundations mastery.
Use a terminal safely, quote arguments, inspect installed help and versions, preserve evidence, and avoid destructive host operations.
deviceConstraints: Modern browser; all learner Git and shell operations run in a deterministic disposable simulator and never reach the host, network, credentials, or real repositories.
accessibilityAssumptions: Every graph has an ordered text equivalent; terminal output, topology choices, and evidence checks support keyboard-only and screen-reader use.

placementEvidence: A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Operate Git working tree, index, commits, branches, remotes, conflicts, tags, revisions, undo, rebase, hooks, and collaboration at Git Foundations mastery.

A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Use a terminal safely, quote arguments, inspect installed help and versions, preserve evidence, and avoid destructive host operations.
completionEvidence: Complete and defend all 5 stakeholder projects against their acceptance constraints and rubrics.
Pass the cumulative performance examination at or above 85 percent with changed-case evidence.

includes: Advanced Git 2.55 reference and object model, revision algebra, storage, reflogs, recovery, layered undo, merge topology, conflict stages, rerere, rewriting, replay, bisect, worktrees, refspecs, maintainer workflows, signatures, submodules, partial clone, sparse checkout, attributes, filters, trust, maintenance, bundles, SHA-256 transition, and disaster defense
Safe stateful repository simulation with changed graphs, exact commands, observable state transitions, causal diagnosis, and recovery verification
Five cumulative production repository projects and a performance-based certification examination
excludes: Hosting-provider UI memorization, live credential use, live remote mutation, executing cloned hooks or repository programs, and unreviewed history filtering on real repositories
Git Foundations topics except explicit retrieval and deeper mechanisms required by an advanced operation

sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves Git Foundations safety, worktree/index/ref/graph distinctions, accessibility, security, testing, and evidence habits before adding one advanced boundary.
All browser Git work is deterministic simulation only. No learner command reaches host Git, files outside the scenario, credentials, hooks, network remotes, or executable repository content.

Passing work requires exact before and after refs, graph and tree evidence, changed-case behavior, failure diagnosis, recovery anchor, residual risk, and live-environment transfer limits appropriate to the competency.

competencies.statement: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
misconceptions: Every ref is a loose text file under .git/refs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.

competencies.statement: Resolve revision names and disambiguate refs, object IDs, paths, reflog selectors, and peel operators before an operation.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve revision names and disambiguate refs, object IDs, paths, reflog selectors, and peel operators before an operation.

competencies.statement: Construct inclusive, exclusive, symmetric-difference, ancestry, parent, and path-limited revision sets for an exact question.

competencies.statement: Use parent selection, first-parent history, generation, and merge-base evidence without confusing display order with causality.

competencies.statement: Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare signed commits, tags, merges, and pushes by what is covered, who verifies them, and which server or client policy applies.

competencies.statement: Fetch and push tags explicitly, detect movement and namespace collisions, handle deletion, and apply immutable-release policy without assuming universal enforcement.
misconceptions: Tags fetched once are immutable locally and remotely unless the object database is corrupt.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fetch and push tags explicitly, detect movement and namespace collisions, handle deletion, and apply immutable-release policy without assuming universal enforcement.

competencies.statement: Bind reviewed source, verified tag target, clean reproducible build, artifact digest, attestation, publication record, and rollback ref.
misconceptions: A valid signed tag proves which artifact bytes users received and that the build was reproducible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind reviewed source, verified tag target, clean reproducible build, artifact digest, attestation, publication record, and rollback ref.

competencies.statement: Explain gitlink entries, superproject commits, submodule repositories, checked-out commits, .gitmodules, local config, and detached submodule HEAD.
misconceptions: A submodule stores another repository and its full branch state inside the superproject commit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain gitlink entries, superproject commits, submodule repositories, checked-out commits, .gitmodules, local config, and detached submodule HEAD.

competencies.statement: Predict init, update, sync, set-url, set-branch, recursive, remote, merge, rebase, and shallow behavior across configuration layers.
misconceptions: git submodule update --remote always records and publishes a new superproject gitlink automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict init, update, sync, set-url, set-branch, recursive, remote, merge, rebase, and shallow behavior across configuration layers.

competencies.statement: Stage gitlink changes, publish dependency commits before superproject refs, review nested diffs, and verify that every referenced object is reachable.
misconceptions: Publishing the superproject first guarantees the referenced submodule commit is available to every clone.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Stage gitlink changes, publish dependency commits before superproject refs, review nested diffs, and verify that every referenced object is reachable.

competencies.statement: Treat URLs, protocols, hooks, recursive commands, credentials, relative paths, and untrusted nested code as explicit trust boundaries.
misconceptions: .gitmodules is inert data and cannot influence protocols, paths, credentials, or recursive execution.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat URLs, protocols, hooks, recursive commands, credentials, relative paths, and untrusted nested code as explicit trust boundaries.

competencies.statement: Compare submodules with package managers, subtree merges, vendoring, monorepos, and build-time fetches by reproducibility, ownership, atomicity, and operations.
misconceptions: Submodules are always obsolete because package managers can represent every source dependency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare submodules with package managers, subtree merges, vendoring, monorepos, and build-time fetches by reproducibility, ownership, atomicity, and operations.

competencies.statement: Explain promisor remotes, promised objects, filter specifications, lazy fetching, missing-object tolerance, and command compatibility.
misconceptions: A blobless clone is shallow history with old commits removed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain promisor remotes, promised objects, filter specifications, lazy fetching, missing-object tolerance, and command compatibility.

competencies.statement: Compare shallow clone, partial clone, sparse checkout, sparse index, single branch, and reference repositories by omitted data and semantic limits.
misconceptions: Shallow, partial, and sparse clones omit the same data and differ only in command syntax.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare shallow clone, partial clone, sparse checkout, sparse index, single branch, and reference repositories by omitted data and semantic limits.

competencies.statement: Use cone patterns, non-cone patterns, set, add, reapply, disable, skip-worktree state, and sparse index while detecting vivified paths.
misconceptions: Skip-worktree is a promise that Git will never materialize or inspect the path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use cone patterns, non-cone patterns, set, add, reapply, disable, skip-worktree state, and sparse index while detecting vivified paths.

competencies.statement: Measure clone transfer, object count, index size, status latency, checkout, fetch, build input, cache, and developer task before choosing an optimization.
misconceptions: Repository size alone identifies the optimization and makes task-level measurements unnecessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure clone transfer, object count, index size, status latency, checkout, fetch, build input, cache, and developer task before choosing an optimization.

competencies.statement: Verify required commands and tools against changed working sets, define full-object release gates, preserve an offline fallback, and document experimental limits.
misconceptions: If sparse status is fast, builds and releases necessarily have every required file and object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify required commands and tools against changed working sets, define full-object release gates, preserve an offline fallback, and document experimental limits.

competencies.statement: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.
misconceptions: Attributes use the same pattern semantics and precedence as .gitignore.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.

competencies.statement: Design text, eol, working-tree-encoding, binary, and renormalization policy with byte-level and cross-platform verification.
misconceptions: Setting text=auto rewrites all existing index entries and safely fixes line endings without review.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design text, eol, working-tree-encoding, binary, and renormalization policy with byte-level and cross-platform verification.

competencies.statement: Configure diff drivers, textconv, binary markers, merge drivers, recursive behavior, and conflict-marker size without treating presentation as storage.
misconceptions: A custom diff driver changes stored blob bytes and therefore defines merge correctness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure diff drivers, textconv, binary markers, merge drivers, recursive behavior, and conflict-marker size without treating presentation as storage.

competencies.statement: Threat-model clean, smudge, and process filters; define required-filter failure, idempotence, delayed output, secrets, and missing-tool behavior.
misconceptions: A clean filter may safely discard source because smudge can always reconstruct it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Threat-model clean, smudge, and process filters; define required-filter failure, idempotence, delayed output, secrets, and missing-tool behavior.

competencies.statement: Choose ordinary Git, LFS-style pointers, release artifacts, package registries, or external storage from review, locking, retention, availability, and migration needs.
misconceptions: Any large file automatically belongs in a pointer-based extension regardless of access and history needs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose ordinary Git, LFS-style pointers, release artifacts, package registries, or external storage from review, locking, retention, availability, and migration needs.

competencies.statement: Inspect configuration origin, scope, type, includes, conditional includes, worktree config, environment, and command overrides before trusting behavior.
misconceptions: git config --list reports values in decisive precedence order, so origin and scope are unnecessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect configuration origin, scope, type, includes, conditional includes, worktree config, environment, and command overrides before trusting behavior.

competencies.statement: Explain protected configuration and safe.directory ownership exceptions without using broad wildcards to conceal unsafe shared repositories.
misconceptions: safe.directory=* changes filesystem ownership and therefore repairs unsafe repository permissions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain protected configuration and safe.directory ownership exceptions without using broad wildcards to conceal unsafe shared repositories.

competencies.statement: Map client and server hook timing, working directory, environment, parameters, exit behavior, bypass paths, distribution, and enforcement limits.
misconceptions: A committed hooks directory creates an unavoidable server-enforced policy for every contributor.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map client and server hook timing, working directory, environment, parameters, exit behavior, bypass paths, distribution, and enforcement limits.

competencies.statement: Choose credential helpers, URL matching, useHttpPath, askpass, environment, token scope, and erasure behavior without writing secrets into repository state.
misconceptions: Credential helpers match only host names and never vary by protocol, username, path, or configured context.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose credential helpers, URL matching, useHttpPath, askpass, environment, token scope, and erasure behavior without writing secrets into repository state.

competencies.statement: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.
misconceptions: Git never executes repository-controlled programs until after the user explicitly runs a build.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.

competencies.statement: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.
misconceptions: git gc only compresses reachable data and can never expire recovery objects or race another process.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.

competencies.statement: Configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.
misconceptions: Enabling every maintenance task at the shortest interval always improves repository performance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.

competencies.statement: Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.
misconceptions: Commit graphs and Bloom filters change reachability truth rather than accelerate selected queries.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.

competencies.statement: Evaluate built-in fsmonitor, untracked cache, split index, sparse index, preload index, and filesystem support using correctness-first benchmarks.
misconceptions: Filesystem monitoring is a universal correctness layer and never needs platform or invalidation checks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate built-in fsmonitor, untracked cache, split index, sparse index, preload index, and filesystem support using correctness-first benchmarks.

competencies.statement: Create full and incremental bundles, declare prerequisites, verify connectivity, restore refs into a fresh repository, and distinguish bundles from complete operational backup.
misconceptions: A successfully created bundle necessarily contains every ref, reflog, hook, config, LFS object, and worktree needed for recovery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create full and incremental bundles, declare prerequisites, verify connectivity, restore refs into a fresh repository, and distinguish bundles from complete operational backup.

competencies.statement: Explain SHA-1 and SHA-256 repository object formats, repository initialization choice, ID length, storage identity, and why algorithms are not a cosmetic display option.
misconceptions: Changing objectFormat rewrites only configuration while preserving every existing object ID.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain SHA-1 and SHA-256 repository object formats, repository initialization choice, ID length, storage identity, and why algorithms are not a cosmetic display option.

competencies.statement: Evaluate compatibility object formats, translation tables, protocol exchange, clone and fetch interoperability, tooling assumptions, and repository immutability limits.
misconceptions: A compatibility object format lets one repository switch storage hashes back and forth without rewriting objects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate compatibility object formats, translation tables, protocol exchange, clone and fetch interoperability, tooling assumptions, and repository immutability limits.

competencies.statement: Inventory scripts, parsers, databases, APIs, hooks, CI, hosting, and documentation for Git 3.0 breaking defaults, long IDs, and version-gated behavior.
misconceptions: If ordinary porcelain commands work, the full toolchain is ready for SHA-256 and Git 3.0.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory scripts, parsers, databases, APIs, hooks, CI, hosting, and documentation for Git 3.0 breaking defaults, long IDs, and version-gated behavior.

competencies.statement: Triage ref, object, pack, index, worktree, remote, credential, and artifact damage; preserve evidence; rebuild from trusted copies; and verify exact losses.
misconceptions: Fetching from origin is a complete repair because remotes necessarily contain every local ref, object, and release artifact.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Triage ref, object, pack, index, worktree, remote, credential, and artifact damage; preserve evidence; rebuild from trusted copies; and verify exact losses.

competencies.statement: Defend repository policy, topology, recovery, scale, trust, release identity, maintenance, migration, drills, residual risks, and owner handoff under changed constraints.
misconceptions: A successful restore drill proves future incidents will have identical data, access, timing, and failure modes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend repository policy, topology, recovery, scale, trust, release identity, maintenance, migration, drills, residual risks, and owner handoff under changed constraints.

learningDesign.retainedPractice: Retrieve and correctly reuse gita-ref-symbolic-pseudoref, gita-readonly-forensic-baseline, gita-object-inspection-batch, gita-lost-tip-discovery, gita-restore-source-staged-worktree, gita-merge-base-multiple, gita-merge-result-defense, gita-rerere-record-reuse, gita-interactive-todo-actions, gita-cherry-pick-mainline-empty, gita-stash-object-model, gita-wip-recovery-policy, gita-bisect-skip-flaky-merge, gita-worktree-branch-exclusivity, gita-fetch-negotiation-prune-tags, gita-stacked-change-dependencies, gita-collaboration-policy-defense, gita-tag-publication-movement, gita-submodule-change-publication, gita-shallow-partial-sparse-distinction, gita-attribute-precedence-query, gita-large-content-policy, gita-credential-helper-boundary, gita-commitgraph-bloom-midx, gita-compat-object-format-transition, gita-production-repository-defense before adding or assessing the changed requirement.
learningDesign.learnerArtifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs.

projects.title: Repository Recovery and Forensics Runbook
projects.userNeed: The team needs a nondestructive object and reflog investigation, retained recovery refs, layer-correct undo, changed-behavior validation, and an accessible evidence timeline.
constraints: Every input, assumption, transformation, and result is traceable.
Invalid, boundary, and changed cases are tested instead of silently discarded.
The result is understandable and useful to its named stakeholder.
